commands.repair_reason: Needs maintainer/security review and contributor real behavior proof before merge; the protected label and proof gate make this a human review path, not an automated repair job.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete security-boundary concern by allowing npm fallback after broad ClawHub artifact request failures.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve ClawHub moderation failures — src/plugins/clawhub.ts:1157-1158 (sha=e5ba5e9b0721547f1acd0fd6faef2952792d037f)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds `ARTIFACT_UNAVAILABLE` handling so official ClawHub install and update failures can fall back to npm, updates related tests and changelog, and refreshes Codex prompt snapshots.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path for the review finding: a non-404 `ClawHubRequestError` during artifact download becomes `ARTIFACT_UNAVAILABLE`, then the update/onboarding/doctor fallback predicates allow npm fallback. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer/security review and contributor real behavior proof before merge; the protected label and proof gate make this a human review path, not an automated repair job.

target.title: fix: fall back from official ClawHub artifact blocks
target.body:
Summary
- Add ClawHub artifact-unavailable handling for official plugin update/install paths.
- Fall back trusted official ClawHub updates to npm only when recorded ClawHub provenance is official `https://clawhub.ai`.
- Preserve beta-to-default fallback behavior, dry-run npm version reporting, and clean npm provenance after fallback.
- Refresh generated Codex prompt snapshots that drifted from latest `main`.

Verification
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/update.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.test.ts`
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`
- `codex review --uncommitted`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: official ClawHub artifact blocks now recover through npm without switching custom ClawHub installs.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout with Node 24.11.1, commit `e5ba5e9b076ae5851650cbce854c7fd33f1e6a08`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/update.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout:
```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  216 passed (216)
Duration  2.81s
```
Observed result after fix: official ClawHub artifact errors fall back to npm, beta default fallback uses default npm spec, dry-runs report npm version, and custom ClawHub provenance remains a ClawHub error.
What was not tested: live ClawHub/network install.
target.author: steipete
